# Transparency no longer works with the 1.10.1b update on Linux\#6729

[New issue](https://github.com/login?return_to=https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/issues/6729)

Copy link

[New issue](https://github.com/login?return_to=https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/issues/6729)

Copy link

Closed

Closed

[Transparency no longer works with the 1.10.1b update on Linux](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/issues/6729#top)#6729

Copy link

Labels

[Bug](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22Bug%22)

## Description

[![@Anirudh-K96](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/192990656?u=dc127355324283260ea6c6c0dc0e1ae3b2f2f03f&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/Anirudh-K96)

[Anirudh-K96](https://github.com/Anirudh-K96)

opened [on Mar 22, 2025on Mar 22, 2025](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/issues/6729#issue-2940444212)

Issue body actions

### Captchas

- I have read the instructions.































I have searched existing issues and avoided creating duplicates.































I am not filing an enhancement request.




















To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar.
While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item.
Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.



### What happened?

The transparency (translucency) effect achieved by various userChrome.css like the Nebula project don't seem to work after the 1.10.1b update on Linux anymore. I have confirmed through Discord and Reddit that this issue is specific to Linux users.

This is the specific userChrome.css that I use: [https://github.com/JustAdumbPrsn/Nebula-A-Minimal-Theme-for-Zen-Browser/tree/main](https://github.com/JustAdumbPrsn/Nebula-A-Minimal-Theme-for-Zen-Browser/tree/main) . The issue is not with this specific css though as other userChrome.css files that use transparency don't work as well.

I have the installed Zen through the AUR on Arch Linux.

### Reproducible?

- I have checked that this issue cannot be reproduced on Mozilla Firefox.




















To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar.
While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item.
Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.



### Version

1.10.1b

### What platform are you seeing the problem on?

Other

[![@Zylvo](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7250436?v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/Zylvo)

Yup, arch KDE with better blur, it's not working after update

### Zylvo commented on Mar 23, 2025on Mar 23, 2025

[![@Zylvo](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7250436?v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/Zylvo)

[Zylvo](https://github.com/Zylvo)

[on Mar 23, 2025on Mar 23, 2025](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/issues/6729#issuecomment-2746128636)

Last edited by Zylvo

More actions

Here's quick workaround to that issue, add that at the end of userChrome.css

```
:root {
  --zen-themed-toolbar-bg-transparent: transparent !important;
}
```

light-dark is probably hardcoded somewhere, I will try to look at changes between versions later to inform devs where it is, or maybe someone will be quicker :D

TO DEVELOPERS

I think that's the commit that caused the issue

[`38770d9`](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/commit/38770d9393b9c4c80e508c2f3dc8e99988990076)

src/browser/base/content/zen-styles/zen-browser-ui.css

```
#zen-main-app-wrapper {
-  background: transparent;
+  background: var(--zen-themed-toolbar-bg-transparent);
  overflow: hidden;
```

## Closing this issue as part of a repo cleanup

Due to technical-debt we had in the past, we are currently clearing out old, duplicate, stale, and non-actionable issues to improve issue tracking and make it easier to focus on fixing real bugs.

If this issue is still relevant, please open a new issue using our updated issue template, which will help us categorize and address it more efficiently.

Thanks for your understanding and for helping us improve the project! Þ
